project.name: Zecurity
project.description: Zecurity is a Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) platform composed of a Go control plane (controller, GraphQL API, PKI, policy engine), a React/TypeScript admin UI, and Rust connector, shield, and client daemons that share Protobuf-defined contracts. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.summary: Constructs and pings a redis.Client wrapped as valkeyClient using a parsed URL/Valkey address, returning an error when the server is unreachable.

nodes.name: SetPKCEState
nodes.summary: Stores a PKCE verifier (and optional workspace name) keyed by nonce in Valkey with a short TTL, used to bind a callback to the initiating browser session.

nodes.name: GetAndDeletePKCEState
nodes.summary: Atomically fetches and deletes the stored PKCE state for a nonce, returning ErrNotFound when the key is absent or has already expired.

nodes.name: SetRefreshToken
nodes.summary: SetRefreshToken in valkey.go.

nodes.name: GetRefreshToken
nodes.summary: Retrieves the active refresh-token value bound to a user ID, returning a not-found error when no token exists or it has expired.

nodes.name: DeleteRefreshToken
nodes.summary: DeleteRefreshToken in valkey.go.

nodes.name: valkey_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Valkey wrapper: connection failures, PKCE state round trips, expiry, refresh-token CRUD, and key namespacing by workspace.

nodes.name: TestNewValkeyClient_Success
nodes.summary: Test case: NewValkeyClient Success.

nodes.name: TestNewValkeyClient_BadURL
nodes.summary: Test case: NewValkeyClient BadURL.

nodes.name: TestNewValkeyClient_Unreachable
nodes.summary: Test case: NewValkeyClient Unreachable.

nodes.name: TestSetAndGetPKCEState
nodes.summary: Test case: SetAndGetPKCEState.

nodes.name: TestGetAndDeletePKCEState_Expired

nodes.name: TestSetAndGetPKCEState_WithWorkspaceName
nodes.summary: Test case: SetAndGetPKCEState WithWorkspaceName.

nodes.summary: Test case: SetAndGetRefreshToken.

nodes.name: TestGetRefreshToken_NotFound
nodes.summary: Test case: GetRefreshToken NotFound.

nodes.summary: Test case: RefreshToken Expired.

nodes.name: auth.go
nodes.summary: Gin/HTTP middleware that verifies the JWT Authorization bearer header, parses claims, and injects a TenantContext into the request context; rejects invalid tokens with a 401 JSON response.

nodes.name: Claims
nodes.summary: JWT claims structure with TenantID, Role, Email plus jwt.RegisteredClaims, used by AuthMiddleware to parse incoming bearer tokens.

nodes.name: AuthMiddleware
nodes.summary: HTTP middleware that extracts and verifies the bearer JWT using HS256, parses Claims (tenant_id, role, email), and injects a TenantContext into the request context; emits 401 JSON on any failure.

nodes.name: role.go
nodes.summary: Middleware factory that enforces a minimum role on protected routes by inspecting the TenantContext set by AuthMiddleware and returning 403 when the user lacks the required role.

nodes.name: RequireRole
nodes.summary: Middleware factory enforcing a role hierarchy on protected routes; reads role from TenantContext and returns 403 JSON when the user is below the required level.

nodes.name: session.go
nodes.summary: Placeholder file for future session middleware; currently contains only the package declaration.

nodes.name: workspace.go
nodes.summary: Middleware that guards multi-tenant routes by ensuring the request TenantContext matches the workspace slug in the URL, returning 403 when the user attempts cross-tenant access.

nodes.name: WorkspaceGuard
nodes.summary: Middleware that compares the workspace slug in the URL/header against the tenant slug in the JWT-derived TenantContext, rejecting cross-tenant access with 403 JSON.

nodes.name: user.go
nodes.summary: Domain model for the users table: identity, tenant binding, OIDC provider/subject, role, status, and timestamps mapped with db struct tags.

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: Persisted user row mapping the users table with tenant/role/provider columns and audit timestamps.

nodes.name: workspace.go
nodes.summary: Domain model for the workspaces table: slug, display name, status, optional CA certificate PEM, and audit timestamps mapped with db struct tags.

nodes.name: Workspace
nodes.summary: Persisted workspace row mapping the workspaces table with slug, status, optional CA cert PEM, and audit timestamps.

nodes.name: bootstrap.go
nodes.summary: Provisions or fetches a user's workspace and membership during first login: creates the workspace, PKI roots, and admin user atomically, or returns an existing membership for returning users.

nodes.name: Result
nodes.summary: Lightweight value returned by Bootstrap describing the resolved tenant/user/role for JWT issuance.

nodes.name: Service
nodes.summary: Bootstrap service holding the Postgres pool and PKI service used to provision workspaces and admin users atomically.

nodes.name: Bootstrap
nodes.summary: Looks up the user by provider/provider_sub; on hit it refreshes last_login and returns the existing membership, on miss it delegates to runBootstrapTransaction to create a new workspace.

nodes.name: runBootstrapTransaction
nodes.summary: Single-transaction workspace creation: provisions PKI roots, inserts the workspace, slug, admin user, and membership rows atomically, rolling back on any failure.

nodes.name: runInvitedUserTransaction
nodes.summary: Transaction that adds an invited user to an existing workspace, inserting the user and membership rows under the existing tenant.

nodes.name: slugify
nodes.summary: Normalizes a workspace display name into a URL-safe slug by lowercasing, stripping non-alphanumerics, and collapsing separators to hyphens.

nodes.name: controller.go
nodes.summary: Generates the controller's own short-lived server TLS certificate signed by the in-memory intermediate CA, embedding the controller SPIFFE URI SAN and DNS/IP SANs for the configured hosts.

nodes.name: GenerateControllerServerTLS
nodes.summary: Method on serviceImpl that mints an ephemeral controller server certificate signed by the intermediate CA, adding the global SPIFFE controller URI and host DNS/IP SANs.

nodes.name: crypto.go
nodes.summary: Low-level cryptographic helpers for the PKI subsystem: P-384 ECDSA keypair generation, HKDF-derived AES-GCM private-key encryption/decryption, PEM encoding/decoding, secure random serial numbers, and key zeroing.

nodes.name: generateECKeyPair
nodes.summary: Generates an ECDSA P-384 private key used by all three PKI layers (root, intermediate, workspace CAs).

nodes.name: encryptPrivateKey
nodes.summary: Encrypts a marshaled CA private key with AES-GCM using an HKDF-derived per-context subkey, returning base64 ciphertext and nonce for at-rest storage.

nodes.name: decryptPrivateKey
nodes.summary: Decrypts a previously encryptPrivateKey-stored CA key with AES-GCM using the HKDF context-derived subkey, returning the original DER bytes.

nodes.name: encodeCertToPEM
nodes.summary: Wraps a DER certificate in a PEM CERTIFICATE block for storage and transport.

nodes.name: encodeECPrivateKeyToPEM
nodes.summary: Marshals an ECDSA key to SEC1 DER and wraps it in an EC PRIVATE KEY PEM block.

nodes.name: parseCertFromPEM
nodes.summary: Decodes a PEM block and parses it into an x509.Certificate, validating the PEM type.

nodes.name: newSerialNumber
nodes.summary: Returns a cryptographically random 128-bit positive serial number suitable for an X.509 certificate.

nodes.name: zeroBytes
nodes.summary: Overwrites a byte slice with zeros to scrub sensitive key material from memory.

nodes.name: certValidity
nodes.summary: Returns a NotBefore/NotAfter pair for a certificate of the requested TTL, anchored to current UTC time.

nodes.name: intermediate.go
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the Intermediate CA: creates it on first startup, persists encrypted key material, and loads the in-memory signing key used to sign workspace CAs and the controller TLS cert.

nodes.name: initIntermediateCA
nodes.summary: Idempotently ensures an intermediate CA row exists, generating one if missing, then loads its decrypted private key into the service.

nodes.name: intermediateCAExists
nodes.summary: Returns whether the singleton intermediate_ca row already exists in Postgres.

nodes.name: generateAndStoreIntermediateCA
nodes.summary: Generates a new intermediate CA signed by the root CA, encrypts its key, and inserts the certificate plus encrypted material into Postgres.

nodes.name: loadIntermediateCAIntoMemory
nodes.summary: Reads the encrypted intermediate CA from Postgres, decrypts the private key with the master secret, and caches it for runtime workspace CA signing.

nodes.name: intermediate_integration_test.go
nodes.summary: Integration test that boots the PKI service against a real Postgres (PKI_TEST_DATABASE_URL) and asserts intermediate CA generation, persistence, and reload semantics.

nodes.name: TestIntermediateCAIntegration
nodes.summary: Verifies end-to-end that initIntermediateCA creates, stores, and reloads an intermediate CA against a real database.

nodes.name: root.go
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the singleton self-signed Root CA: ensures one exists in the database on first startup and exposes a loader used when signing the intermediate CA.

nodes.name: initRootCA
nodes.summary: Ensures the singleton Root CA row exists, generating and storing one if missing.

nodes.name: rootCAExists
nodes.summary: Returns whether the singleton root_ca row is already present in Postgres.

nodes.name: generateAndStoreRootCA
nodes.summary: Creates a self-signed ECDSA root certificate, encrypts the private key with the master secret, and persists the row in Postgres.

nodes.name: loadRootCA
nodes.summary: Loads the root CA certificate and decrypts its private key on demand for signing the intermediate CA.

nodes.name: root_integration_test.go
nodes.summary: Integration test for the root CA bootstrap path including database setup helpers that apply migrations to an ephemeral Postgres database.

nodes.name: TestRootCAIntegration
nodes.summary: Exercises generateAndStoreRootCA against a fresh test database and verifies the resulting certificate and stored row are consistent.

nodes.name: mustConnectTestPool
nodes.summary: Test helper that opens a pgxpool connection or fails the test if the database is unreachable.

nodes.name: applyMigration
nodes.summary: Applies the PKI SQL migration file to a test database before the integration test runs.

nodes.name: rootMigrationPath
nodes.summary: Computes the absolute on-disk path to the PKI root migration SQL relative to the test file.

nodes.name: withDatabaseName
nodes.summary: Returns a copy of a Postgres DSN with its database name replaced, used to create per-test isolated databases.

nodes.name: uniqueTestDatabaseName
nodes.summary: Generates a unique database name per test run to prevent cross-test interference.

nodes.name: service.go
nodes.summary: Defines the PKI Service public interface and result structs, plus the serviceImpl that owns the master secret, pgxpool, and in-memory intermediate signing key; provides Init bootstrap with secret-mismatch recovery.

nodes.name: Service
nodes.summary: Public PKI contract abstracting CA implementation details: generate workspace CA, sign/renew connector/shield/client certificates, mint controller server TLS, and generate client CRLs.

nodes.name: WorkspaceCAResult
nodes.summary: Return struct carrying an encrypted workspace CA private key, nonce, certificate PEM, key algorithm, and validity window for atomic DB persistence during workspace bootstrap.

nodes.name: ConnectorCertResult
nodes.summary: Result of signing a connector CSR: the leaf certificate PEM plus workspace and intermediate CA PEM chain, serial, and validity window.

nodes.name: ShieldCertResult
nodes.summary: Result of signing a shield certificate: leaf PEM, workspace and intermediate CA chain, serial, and validity window used by shield mTLS to the connector.

nodes.name: ClientCertResult
nodes.summary: Result of signing a client certificate carrying the leaf, workspace and intermediate CA chain, serial, and validity used for end-user mTLS to RDE.

nodes.name: ControllerServerTLSResult
nodes.summary: In-memory controller server TLS material (certificate and private key PEM with validity) generated at startup and never persisted.

nodes.name: serviceImpl
nodes.summary: Concrete implementation of the PKI Service holding the master secret, pgx pool, and decrypted intermediate signing key; provides PKI reset recovery when the master secret rotates.

nodes.name: intermediateCAState
nodes.summary: Internal pairing of the decoded intermediate certificate and its decrypted ECDSA private key held in serviceImpl for signing.

nodes.name: Init
nodes.summary: Constructs and bootstraps a PKI Service: reads PKI_MASTER_SECRET, ensures root and intermediate CAs exist, and falls back to a reset path if the master secret can no longer decrypt stored material.

nodes.name: tryRecoverFromSecretMismatch
nodes.summary: Detects a master-secret mismatch on init and, when safe (no live workspaces), resets the PKI tables and re-runs bootstrap so dev environments can recover.

nodes.name: isSecretMismatchError
nodes.summary: Classifies an error as caused by AES-GCM decryption failure from the wrong master secret.

nodes.name: canResetPKI
nodes.summary: Returns true only when no workspace CAs exist, gating destructive PKI reset to safe development states.

nodes.name: resetPKI
nodes.summary: Truncates root_ca and intermediate_ca tables under a transaction so a fresh bootstrap can run with a new master secret.

nodes.name: workspace.go
nodes.summary: Per-tenant CA operations: generate a Workspace CA signed by the intermediate, sign and renew connector/shield/client certificates with SPIFFE URI SANs, and emit empty client CRLs.

nodes.name: GenerateWorkspaceCA
nodes.summary: Creates a tenant-scoped workspace CA signed by the intermediate CA, returning the encrypted private key and certificate PEM for caller persistence.

nodes.name: SignConnectorCert
nodes.summary: Signs a connector CSR with the workspace CA, embedding the connector SPIFFE URI SAN and producing a short-lived client certificate plus CA chain.

nodes.name: RenewConnectorCert
nodes.summary: Re-signs a connector certificate using its existing public key and SPIFFE identity to extend validity without re-enrollment.

nodes.name: SignShieldCert
nodes.summary: Signs a shield CSR with the workspace CA and embeds the shield SPIFFE URI used for mTLS to the connector.

nodes.name: RenewShieldCert
nodes.summary: Renews an existing shield certificate, preserving its SPIFFE URI SAN.

nodes.name: SignClientCert
nodes.summary: Signs a client CSR with the workspace CA, embedding the user's client SPIFFE URI SAN for end-user mTLS to the RDE.

nodes.name: GenerateClientCRL
nodes.summary: Produces a workspace-scoped client certificate revocation list (currently empty) signed by the workspace CA for RDE consumption.

nodes.name: workspace_integration_test.go
nodes.summary: Integration test for workspace CA generation and certificate signing with helpers that assert workspace CA result fields and certificate trust chain properties.

nodes.name: TestWorkspaceCAIntegration
nodes.summary: End-to-end test that generates a workspace CA and signs a connector certificate against a real database, asserting the SPIFFE chain.

nodes.name: assertWorkspaceCAResult
nodes.summary: Verifies a WorkspaceCAResult has consistent encrypted key material, certificate PEM, and validity bounds.

nodes.name: assertWorkspaceCert
nodes.summary: Verifies a signed workspace-issued certificate has the expected SPIFFE URI, validity window, and issuer chain.

nodes.name: identity.go
nodes.summary: Product-level identity constants and SPIFFE URI builders for global trust domain, controller, workspaces, connectors, shields, and clients; intentionally not configurable so a compromised config cannot redirect identity trust.

nodes.name: WorkspaceTrustDomain
nodes.summary: Returns the SPIFFE trust domain for a workspace slug as 'ws-<slug>.zecurity.in' — the single canonical builder used by all callers.

nodes.name: ConnectorSPIFFEID
nodes.summary: Builds the full SPIFFE URI for a connector within a workspace trust domain.

nodes.name: ShieldSPIFFEID
nodes.summary: Builds the full SPIFFE URI for a shield within a workspace trust domain.

nodes.name: ClientSPIFFEID
nodes.summary: Builds the full SPIFFE URI for an end-user client within a workspace trust domain.

nodes.name: identity_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying SPIFFE URI builders, trust domain formatting, and global identity constants used by both controller and Rust connector/shield.

nodes.name: TestWorkspaceTrustDomain
nodes.summary: Asserts WorkspaceTrustDomain produces 'ws-<slug>.zecurity.in' for representative slugs.

nodes.name: TestConnectorSPIFFEID
nodes.summary: Asserts ConnectorSPIFFEID emits a correctly formed spiffe:// URI within the workspace trust domain.

nodes.name: TestSPIFFEConstants
nodes.summary: Verifies SPIFFE role-segment and trust-domain constants stay consistent with documented values.

nodes.name: TestSPIFFEControllerIDFormat
nodes.summary: Verifies SPIFFEControllerID is the canonical 'spiffe://zecurity.in/controller/global' URI.

nodes.name: TestTrustDomainSuffixReferencesGlobalDomain
nodes.summary: Guards against drift between SPIFFETrustDomainSuffix and SPIFFEGlobalTrustDomain so workspace SPIFFE URIs always nest under the vendor domain.

nodes.name: pool.go
nodes.summary: Owns the package-level pgxpool.Pool: initializes it from DATABASE_URL with bounded connection limits, pings to verify connectivity, and exposes a clean Close.

nodes.name: Init
nodes.summary: Initializes the global Postgres pool from DATABASE_URL with MaxConns=25 and verifies connectivity before the HTTP server starts.

nodes.name: Close
nodes.summary: Closes the global pgxpool.Pool on shutdown.

nodes.name: tenant.go
nodes.summary: Defines TenantDB: a pgxpool wrapper that panics when TenantContext is missing from ctx, enforcing visible tenant_id scoping in every SQL string rather than auto-rewriting queries.

nodes.name: TenantDB
nodes.summary: Pgx pool wrapper enforcing the presence of a TenantContext in ctx for every QueryRow/Query/Exec/BeginTx call; SQL strings must still include tenant_id parameters explicitly.

nodes.name: NewTenantDB
nodes.summary: Constructs a TenantDB wrapping a pgx pool.

nodes.name: require
nodes.summary: Asserts a TenantContext is present in ctx (panicking via MustGet if not) before any DB call.

nodes.name: QueryRow
nodes.summary: Tenant-aware single-row query; requires TenantContext and delegates to pgxpool.QueryRow.

nodes.name: Query
nodes.summary: Tenant-aware multi-row query; requires TenantContext and delegates to pgxpool.Query.

nodes.name: Exec
nodes.summary: Tenant-aware exec; requires TenantContext and delegates to pgxpool.Exec.

nodes.name: BeginTx
nodes.summary: Begins a pgx transaction after verifying a TenantContext is present in ctx.

nodes.name: RawPool
nodes.summary: Returns the underlying pgxpool.Pool for callers that need direct, tenant-unchecked access (e.g. bootstrap, migrations).

nodes.name: config.go
nodes.summary: Discovery configuration with a 24-hour ScanResultTTL constant used to prune stale scan results.

nodes.name: Config
nodes.summary: Container struct holding the discovery ScanResultTTL.

nodes.name: NewConfig
nodes.summary: Returns the default discovery Config with ScanResultTTL set to 24h.

nodes.name: store.go
nodes.summary: Postgres persistence for resource discovery: per-shield discovered services (upsert/replace/get/delete) and per-request scan results (upsert/get/purge) used by the discovery pipeline.

nodes.name: DiscoveredService
nodes.summary: Row model for a service observed running on a shield: protocol, port, bound IP, service name, first/last-seen timestamps.

nodes.name: ScanResult
nodes.summary: Row model for a single port-scan finding tied to a request and a connector with optional service name and reachability metadata.

nodes.name: UpsertDiscoveredServices
nodes.summary: Transactionally upserts added and removes deleted discovered services for a shield within a single txn.

nodes.name: ReplaceDiscoveredServices
nodes.summary: Replaces the full set of discovered services for a shield with the provided list (delete-then-insert under a transaction).

nodes.name: GetDiscoveredServices
nodes.summary: Returns all currently known DiscoveredService rows for a given shield.

nodes.name: DeleteDiscoveredService
nodes.summary: Removes a single DiscoveredService row identified by shield, protocol, port, and bound IP.

nodes.name: UpsertScanResults
nodes.summary: Persists scan_result rows reported by a connector for a discovery request, deduplicating on natural key.

nodes.name: GetScanResults
nodes.summary: Fetches all ScanResult rows for a given discovery request id.

nodes.name: PurgeScanResults
nodes.summary: Deletes scan_result rows older than the configured ScanResultTTL.

nodes.name: email.go
nodes.summary: Invitation Emailer that sends invitation links via SMTP, falling back to stdout in dev mode when SMTP_HOST is unset.

nodes.name: Emailer
nodes.summary: SMTP sender for invitation emails parameterized at construction with host, port, from, password, and the public base URL embedded in the invitation link.

nodes.name: NewEmailer
nodes.summary: Constructs an Emailer from explicit config values, keeping SMTP credentials out of package-level env reads.

nodes.name: Send
nodes.summary: Sends an invitation email via SMTP (or logs to stdout in dev mode) with the workspace name and a tokenized accept URL.

nodes.name: handler.go
nodes.summary: HTTP handler exposing the invitation REST endpoints (create, get-by-token, accept) wired to the invitation Store and Emailer, including a JSON response shape and JSON error helper.

nodes.name: Handler
nodes.summary: REST handler exposing POST /api/invitations, GET /api/invitations/{token}, and POST /api/invitations/{token}/accept routes registered in main.go.

nodes.name: invitationResponse
nodes.summary: JSON DTO returned by invitation endpoints carrying id, email, workspace info, status, and timestamps.

nodes.name: NewHandler
nodes.summary: Constructs a Handler with the invitation Store and Emailer dependencies.

nodes.name: toResponse
nodes.summary: Maps an internal Invitation row into the public invitationResponse JSON shape.

nodes.name: Create
nodes.summary: Handles POST /api/invitations by creating an invitation row and sending the invite email.

nodes.name: Get
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/invitations/{token} returning invitation status for the accept-page UI; public endpoint.

nodes.name: Accept
nodes.summary: Handles POST /api/invitations/{token}/accept by marking the invitation accepted under the authenticated tenant context.

nodes.name: writeJSONError
nodes.summary: Writes a uniform JSON error response with a given status code from the invitation endpoints.

nodes.name: store.go
nodes.summary: Postgres Store for the invitations table: create with random hex token, lookup by token (with workspace join), and accept under transaction; defines ErrNotFound.

nodes.name: Invitation
nodes.summary: Row model mirroring the invitations table with workspace name populated by joins on Get queries.

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: Owns invitation persistence: CreateInvitation, GetByToken, and AcceptInvitation operations against the pgx pool.

nodes.name: NewStore
nodes.summary: Constructs an invitation Store with the shared pgx pool.

nodes.name: CreateInvitation
nodes.summary: Generates a random hex token and inserts a pending invitation row with expiry into Postgres.

nodes.name: GetByToken
nodes.summary: Returns an Invitation row keyed by token joined with its workspace, or ErrNotFound if missing.

nodes.name: AcceptInvitation
nodes.summary: Marks an invitation accepted under a transaction, attaching the accepting user to the workspace.

nodes.name: lan.go
nodes.summary: Networking helpers: derives the controller's outbound LAN IP, builds gRPC/HTTP listen addresses from it, and classifies loopback addresses.

nodes.name: DetectLANAddr
nodes.summary: Returns the gRPC and HTTP listen addresses built from the detected outbound LAN IP and provided ports.

nodes.name: IsLocalhost
nodes.summary: Returns true when the given host:port resolves to a loopback (localhost, 127.x.x.x, ::1).

nodes.name: DetectLANIP
nodes.summary: Public wrapper around the internal detectLANIP that returns the outbound LAN IP or 'localhost' on failure.

nodes.name: detectLANIP
nodes.summary: Probes the local outbound LAN IP using a dummy UDP dial; falls back to 'localhost' on error.

nodes.name: config.go
nodes.summary: Resource package config carrying the pgx pool injected from main.go for store construction.

nodes.name: Config
nodes.summary: Resource package configuration struct carrying the pgx pool dependency.

nodes.name: NewConfig
nodes.summary: Constructs a resource Config from a pgxpool.

nodes.name: store.go
nodes.summary: Resource (protected service) persistence and lifecycle: create/update/soft-delete, auto-match a connector's shield from host, mark protecting/unprotecting, track shield apply acknowledgements, and surface pending instructions for stream delivery.

nodes.name: Row
nodes.summary: Full resource record joined with shield and remote network names, including protection status, pending action, error message, and lifecycle timestamps.

nodes.name: PendingRow
nodes.summary: Minimal resource record used to push pending apply/remove instructions to a shield via the connector heartbeat stream.

nodes.name: CreateInput
nodes.summary: DTO carrying caller-supplied fields for creating a new resource row (name, host, protocol, port range, etc.).

nodes.name: UpdateInput
nodes.summary: DTO carrying optional fields for updating an existing resource row.

nodes.name: scanRow
nodes.summary: Scans a pgx row into the joined resource Row struct used by all read paths.

nodes.name: AutoMatchShield
nodes.summary: Resolves the connector and shield that should manage a host by matching against discovered services, returning shield + connector IDs.

nodes.name: Create
nodes.summary: Inserts a new resource row in 'pending' state with auto-matched shield and connector for the host.

nodes.name: GetByID
nodes.summary: Returns a single resource Row by id with joined shield and remote network names.

nodes.name: GetByRemoteNetwork
nodes.summary: Returns all resource Rows scoped to a remote network used by the GraphQL resolver.

nodes.name: GetAll
nodes.summary: Returns all resource Rows for the tenant context.

nodes.name: GetPendingForShield
nodes.summary: Returns the PendingRow set for a shield that needs apply or remove instructions pushed via the connector stream.

nodes.name: Update
nodes.summary: Applies a partial UpdateInput to an existing resource row using dynamic SET-clause building via joinSets.

nodes.name: joinSets
nodes.summary: Helper that joins partial SQL SET fragments and parameter lists for dynamic UPDATE statements.

nodes.name: MarkProtecting
nodes.summary: Transitions a resource into the 'protecting' state with pending_action='apply' to schedule shield enforcement.

nodes.name: MarkUnprotecting
nodes.summary: Sets pending_action='remove' so the shield will tear down its nftables rules on the next stream cycle.

nodes.name: SoftDelete
nodes.summary: Soft-deletes a resource row, leaving it in the database for audit while removing it from active queries.

nodes.name: RecordAck
nodes.summary: Persists a shield apply/remove acknowledgement, updating applied_at, last_verified_at, status, and any error_message.

nodes.name: collectRows
nodes.summary: Iterates a pgx.Rows result through scanRow to build a slice of resource Rows.

nodes.name: context.go
nodes.summary: Defines TenantContext (tenant_id, user_id, role, email), an unexported context key, and Set/Get/MustGet helpers used by every resolver and DB call to enforce tenant isolation.

nodes.name: TenantContext
nodes.summary: Verified per-request identity holding workspace UUID, user UUID, role (admin/member/viewer), and authenticated email; populated by AuthMiddleware after JWT verification.

nodes.name: Set
nodes.summary: Attaches a TenantContext to a context.Context using an unexported key, called only by AuthMiddleware after JWT verification.

nodes.name: Get
nodes.summary: Returns the TenantContext from ctx if present, used by handlers where absence is a valid case (e.g. public routes).

nodes.name: MustGet
nodes.summary: Returns the TenantContext from ctx, panicking when absent — used in resolvers and repository functions where missing context is always a bug.
